instruction:
We now define an algorithm: The definition of a(n) is the least nontrivial exponent e such that the decimal representation of n is a substring of the decimal representation of n raised to the power of e. Given the input x_list (a series of values): [78, 79, 80, 81, 82, 83, 84, 85, 86, 87], determine the corresponding output sequence y_list.

Options:
A. [16, 8, 2, 13, 19, 11, 8, 28, 16, 4]
B. [19, 10, 4, 9, 12, 6, 11, 25, 19, 5]
C. [14, 6, 0, 15, 21, 2, 7, 30, 14, 2]
D. [22, 12, 6, 7, 15, 5, 12, 23, 23, 8]
E. [18, 9, 3, 8, 13, 7, 10, 24, 20, 6]
F. [23, 14, 8, 11, 17, 9, 14, 26, 18, 10]
G. [20, 13, 7, 10, 16, 8, 13, 21, 21, 9]
H. [21, 11, 5, 6, 14, 4, 11, 22, 22, 7]
I. [17, 15, 9, 12, 18, 10, 0, 27, 17, 11]
J. [15, 7, 1, 14, 20, 3, 9, 29, 15, 3]
